zotero.abstractNote: High-contrast imaging (HCI) is a key technique for the direct detection and characterization of exoplanets. However, the raw data is dominated by quasi-static speckle noise, which is several orders of magnitude brighter than the planetary signal. Current post-processing algorithms rely on the assumption that the planetary signal and the noise can be separated based on their different temporal evolution. In this work, we propose a novel approach to HCI post-processing based on causal inference. We model the noise as a causal process and use physical constraints to disentangle the planetary signal from the noise. We demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms on both synthetic and real data.
